{{Infobox group
| name = Fishers
| introduced = Soulsmith
}}

''See also: [[Fishers Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The Fishers are a sect specializing in tracking, wilderness navigation, and the extraction of [[natural treasures]]. They serve as a primary supplier for [[Soulsmiths]] within the [[Five Factions Alliance]], providing them with [[Remnant]] parts and bindings. Additionally, they provide refiners with rare medicinal ingredients and components for elixirs sourced from [[sacred beasts]]. While highly skilled at harvesting resources, their [[madra]] is not ideally suited for combat, which sometimes forces them to abandon valuable finds. Their unique use of force-based [[madra]] allows them to pull objects toward them as if using invisible lines. Members of the sect typically wear modest, worn clothing consisting of brown robes, sandals, and wide-brimmed reed hats for sun protection. Their signature weapon is a large, crescent-shaped bladed hook that is sharp on the inside; some members, such as Gesha and the [[First Fisher]], utilize two of these weapons in combat. The sect maintains specific territories, including areas characterized by unfinished wooden structures. The [[First Fisher]] is a prominent and wealthy individual in the Wilds, known for maintaining a strong reputation among other factions.}}

== Soulsmith ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The Fishers are a sect that follows the [[Path of the Fisherman]], specializing in tracking, navigating the wilderness, and extracting [[natural treasures]] such as [[sacred beasts]] and [[Remnants]]. As ancestral enemies of the [[Sandvipers]], they adhere to the law of the Wilds by taking whatever they can keep, which included the capture of Sandviper [[miners]]. This group, which utilizes hooks and includes [[Ragahn]] and [[Lindon]]'s companion, encountered [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] and guided them toward a tall building. Having helped navigate most of the doors in the [[Ruins]], the Fishers proved they honor their debts by providing [[Yerin]] with a new sacred artist's robe and a small cottage.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Fishers]] are the ancestral enemy of the [[Sandvipers]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c7">''Soulsmith'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Fishers]] are a group that [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] encounter.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c8">''Soulsmith'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Fishers]] are a group that captured Sandviper [[miners]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Fishers]] follow the law of the Wilds: you take whatever you can keep.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Fishers]] led [[Lindon]] to a tall building.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c10">''Soulsmith'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Fishers]] follow the [[Path of the Fisherman]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Fishers]] are a sect that hunts [[Remnants]] and [[sacred beasts]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c13">''Soulsmith'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Fishers]] are experts at tracking, navigating the wilderness, and extracting [[natural treasures]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Fishers]] honor their debts.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c15">''Soulsmith'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Fishers]] provided [[Yerin]] with a new sacred artist's robe.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c16">''Soulsmith'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Fishers]] have helped navigate most of the doors in the [[Ruins]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c17">''Soulsmith'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Fishers]] are a group that uses hooks.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c18">''Soulsmith'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Fishers]] are a group that includes [[Ragahn]] and [[Lindon]]'s companion.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c19">''Soulsmith'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Fishers]] provided [[Yerin]] with a little cottage.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c20">''Soulsmith'', chapter 20</ref>}}

== Blackflame ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Scouts from the [[Fishers]] have spotted Arelius banners.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c3">''Blackflame'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Fishers]] can advance [[Lindon]] past the [[Iron stage]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c5">''Blackflame'', chapter 5</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Fishers]] were hiding nearby.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 14</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|"Brother Jai Long," the Sandviper said, "these Fishers captured some of our miners on their return from the Ruins."<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c8" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|"It’s the law of the Wilds, Sandviper. You take whatever you can keep."<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c8" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Groups]]
[[Category:Soulsmith]]
[[Category:Blackflame]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle groups]]
